query: Best-in-class Rust code architecture for Polish — robust, modular, adapter-based, strongly-typed, contributor-friendly
refinedQuery: Recommend complete Rust workspace architecture for Polish (Windows maintenance suite) covering architectural style, crate layout, type-safety patterns, plugin/adapter registration, error handling discipline, testing strategy, and contributor docs.
sections: Error handling discipline (thiserror in libs, anyhow in bins)

findings.subtopic: Hexagonal architecture is the recommended style
findings.claim: Multiple production Rust projects (Banker, hexagonal-rust, blueprint-hexagonal-rust, Hextacy) converge on the hexagonal/ports-and-adapters split: pure domain crate + application crate + adapter crate + bin crates. Polish should adopt this with crates polish-core (domain), polish-app (use cases), polish-adapters (infra), polish-ipc (wire types), polish-test-fixtures, plus 4 binaries.

Typestate encodes state in the type (Cleanup<Scanned>, Cleanup<Previewed>, Cleanup<Executed>, Cleanup<Restorable>) so invalid transitions fail to compile. Zero runtime cost — marker structs are 0-sized, all checking happens at compile time. Sealed traits prevent external crates from adding states. Maps directly to Polish's scan→preview→execute→quarantine→restore flow.

findings.subtopic: Distributed category registration via inventory crate
findings.claim: dtolnay/inventory provides typed distributed plugin registration with no central registry file. Contributors add a new file in polish-adapters/src/categories/<id>.rs with inventory::submit!(CategoryEntry {...}); engine collects all entries at startup via inventory::iter. Eliminates merge conflicts on contributor PRs. Works across statically and dynamically linked code. Production-stable; maintained by dtolnay (most respected Rust library author). linkme is the alternative without life-before-main.

findings.subtopic: thiserror in libs + anyhow in bins is the convergent best practice
findings.claim: 2026 sources all agree: thiserror for libraries (callers need typed variants to match on), anyhow for application binaries (chain + display friendly). Use #[source] / #[from] to preserve causes. #[error(transparent)] for forwarding. Never use anyhow in public library APIs.

findings.subtopic: Trait objects + mockall for testing adapter architecture
findings.claim: Define ports as traits in polish-core, implement in polish-adapters, mock with mockall in polish-test-fixtures. Use dyn dispatch (trait objects) for collections of heterogeneous categories; generic dispatch for hot paths. Mockall auto-derives mocks from traits.

findings.subtopic: entrait as optional DI friction reducer
findings.claim: entrait crate auto-generates trait + impl + mock from a single function definition. Reduces boilerplate vs hand-rolled traits + mockall. Trade-off: macro magic + steeper learning curve. Recommendation: start with explicit traits + mockall (transparent, teachable); adopt entrait only if boilerplate becomes painful at scale.

keyInsights: Hexagonal architecture is overkill for tiny projects but exactly the right cost for Polish's multi-year + multi-binary + open-core + contributor-extensible profile.

Typestate pushes Polish's cleanup-pipeline invariants from runtime checks to compile-time guarantees. Zero runtime cost. Catches whole classes of bugs (execute-before-preview, restore-before-quarantine) at cargo build time.
inventory crate makes contributor onboarding for new cleanup categories trivially merge-conflict-free — one new file, zero central registry edits.
Strict layered crate split + cargo-deny dependency direction rules + clippy denylist convert the 'rules contributors must remember' into 'rules CI rejects.' Architecture survives turnover.
Each port = one trait in polish-core. Each adapter = one impl in polish-adapters. Each mock = one mockall::automock derive. Pattern is repeatable for every new I/O boundary.
redFlags: Hexagonal architecture introduces meaningful upfront cost (crate split, trait definitions, dep wiring). Premature optimization for a project that doesn't ship is real risk — must commit to the build cadence or this becomes wasted effort.
inventory uses life-before-main initialization which is platform-sensitive (works on Windows but could surprise contributors who haven't seen this pattern).
Tauri 2 hexagonal split for the UI side was NOT covered in this research — needs separate research before polish-ui is built.
Strict no-unwrap / no-panic lints catch real bugs but slow initial development. Worth it; preview before enforcing.
gaps: Tauri 2 + hexagonal pattern not researched — Tauri commands as inbound adapters likely answer but unconfirmed.
CQRS / event-sourcing for .pq quarantine state not researched. Could be elegant but adds complexity.
async vs sync ports not deeply researched — sync examples dominated. Polish probably wants async via tokio + async-trait or stable async fn in traits (Rust 1.75+).
Tauri command boundary serialization vs polish-ipc named-pipe serialization — should they share serde types from polish-ipc? Likely yes but not verified.
Performance benchmark of dyn Category dispatch in hot scan loops — assumed negligible (I/O-dominated) but not measured.
